questions.explanation: Helen Keller lost her ability to see and hear when she was very young due to an illness. She was both blind and deaf.
hints: Think about which senses Helen lost

questions.explanation: Anne Sullivan was Helen Keller's teacher. She taught Helen to communicate using sign language spelled into her palm.
hints: Her teacher's first name was Anne

questions.questionText: The first word Helen understood was:
questions.explanation: The first word Helen understood was 'water'. Anne Sullivan spelled W-A-T-E-R into Helen's hand while water flowed over her other hand.

questions.questionText: Helen Keller grew up to become a famous:
questions.explanation: Helen Keller became a famous author and speaker. She wrote many books and gave speeches to inspire others.

questions.questionText: The poem 'Don't Be Afraid of the Dark' teaches us about:
options: Being brave and not fearing darkness
questions.explanation: The poem teaches us to be brave and not fear the dark. It encourages courage and facing our fears.
hints: What does 'Don't be afraid' tell us to do?

questions.explanation: An adjective is a describing word. It tells us more about a noun - like beautiful, tall, brave, dark.

questions.questionText: Which word is an adjective in: 'Helen was a brave girl'?
questions.explanation: 'Brave' is the adjective because it describes what kind of girl Helen was. Adjectives describe nouns.

questions.questionText: True or False: Helen Keller went to college.
questions.explanation: True! Helen Keller graduated from Radcliffe College. She was the first deaf-blind person to earn a college degree.
hints: Helen achieved many things despite her challenges

questions.questionText: Which adjectives describe Helen Keller best?
questions.explanation: Helen Keller was brave and determined. Despite not being able to see or hear, she worked hard to learn and achieve great things.

questions.questionText: Helen Keller's story teaches us that:
options: Nothing is impossible with hard work
questions.explanation: Helen Keller's story teaches us that nothing is impossible with hard work and determination. She overcame huge challenges to achieve great things.
hints: What is the main lesson from her life?
